segments.text: After this Job opened his mouth, and cursed the day of his birth.
Job answered:
Let the day perish in which I was born, the night which said, ‘There is a boy conceived.’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
Let that day be darkness. Don’t let God from above seek for it, 
neither let the light shine on it.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
Let darkness and the shadow of death claim it for their own. Let a cloud dwell on it. 
Let all that makes the day black terrify it.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
As for that night, let thick darkness seize on it. Let it not rejoice among the days of the year. 
Let it not come into the number of the months.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Behold, let that night be barren. Let no joyful voice come therein.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Let them curse it who curse the day, who are ready to rouse up leviathan.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
Let the stars of its twilight be dark. Let it look for light, but have none, 
neither let it see the eyelids of the morning,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: because it didn’t shut up the doors of my mother’s womb, nor did it hide trouble from my eyes.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: “Why didn’t I die from the womb? Why didn’t I give up the spirit when my mother bore me?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why did the knees receive me? Or why the breast, that I should nurse?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For now I should have lain down and been quiet. I should have slept, then I would have been at rest,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: with kings and counselors of the earth, who built up waste places for themselves;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: or with princes who had gold, who filled their houses with silver;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: or as a hidden untimely birth I had not been, as infants who never saw light.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: There the wicked cease from troubling. There the weary are at rest.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: There the prisoners are at ease together. They don’t hear the voice of the taskmaster.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The small and the great are there. The servant is free from his master.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: “Why is light given to him who is in misery, life to the bitter in soul,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: who long for death, but it doesn’t come; and dig for it more than for hidden treasures,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: who rejoice exceedingly, and are glad, when they can find the grave?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why is light given to a man whose way is hidden, whom God has hedged in?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For my sighing comes before I eat. My groanings are poured out like water.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For the thing which I fear comes on me, that which I am afraid of comes to me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I am not at ease, neither am I quiet, neither do I have rest; but trouble comes.
segments.speaker: Job
